original_abstract: This article examines the reform of Polish water law, focusing on the fundamental objectives behind the proposed new Water Law Act. Key areas of analysis include the reorganization of administrative competencies, the separation of infrastructure management from water resource management, and the implementation of a catchment-oriented policy and simplified financing models. By conducting a comparative study of existing regulations and proposed legislative changes, the authors highlight systemic flaws within the current legal framework and identify their root causes. Ultimately, the paper seeks to evaluate whether the proposed legal solutions offer a genuine remedy to the persistent challenges facing the national water management system.
humanized_abstract: Paper First Title Reform of Polish water law – goals of the new law on water Author Ewelina BRYLA Magdalena LEJA Citation Acta Geophysica 61 (1) Pages 55-77 Published OnlineFirst: 16 September 2014 Abstract In this paper we present the reform of Polish water law which has been ongoing in recent years, focusing on the main goals of the proposed new law – Water Law Act. The subject matter of our analysis are, in particular, the fragmentation of managerial competences at various levels of governance, the clear separation of infrastructure management from water resource management, and a catchment-based policy with simpler methods of financial coverage of water investments. We present, based on our own comparative analysis of the existing law and new regulations, the factors which are responsible for the present deficiencies of water law and we discuss whether the law as proposed will indeed constitute an effective method of improving the performance of the national water management system.
